record.field: PhotoPrism is a self-hosted, AI-powered photo and video manager that keeps your entire library private on your own server.

PhotoPrism is a self-hosted, AI-powered photo and video manager that keeps your entire library private on your own server.

It indexes large collections of photos and videos and makes them searchable through automatic, on-device labeling, face recognition, and location-based browsing. Built in Go with a Vue progressive web app, it targets photographers, families, and power users who want cloud-grade organization and search without uploading anything to a third party.

Key features
  • Automatic content labeling and face recognition that run locally for full privacy
  • Powerful search by content, people, places, dates, colors, and metadata
  • RAW image, video, live photo, and PDF support with metadata editing
  • World maps with privacy-preserving geocoding to browse photos by location
  • Progressive web app plus WebDAV access for syncing and direct file management
  • Multi-architecture Docker images for AMD, Intel, and ARM (Raspberry Pi, Apple Silicon)
Best for

Individuals, families, and photographers who want to self-host and organize large photo and video libraries with AI-assisted search while keeping their data fully under their own control.

services: photoprism: image: photoprism/photoprism:latest stop_grace_period: 10s depends_on: - mariadb security_opt: - seccomp:unconfined - apparmor:unconfined environment: PHOTOPRISM_ADMIN_USER: admin PHOTOPRISM_ADMIN_PASSWORD: {{ admin_password }} PHOTOPRISM_AUTH_MODE: password PHOTOPRISM_SITE_URL: http://localhost:2342/ PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_TLS: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DEFAULT_TLS: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_ORIGINALS_LIMIT: 5000 PHOTOPRISM_HTTP_COMPRESSION: gzip PHOTOPRISM_LOG_LEVEL: info PHOTOPRISM_READONLY: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_EXPERIMENTAL: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_CHOWN: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_WEBDAV: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_SETTINGS: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_TENSORFLOW: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_FACES: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_CLASSIFICATION: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_VECTORS: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_DISABLE_RAW: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_RAW_PRESETS: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_SIDECAR_YAML: 'true' PHOTOPRISM_BACKUP_ALBUMS: 'true' PHOTOPRISM_BACKUP_DATABASE: 'true' PHOTOPRISM_BACKUP_SCHEDULE: daily PHOTOPRISM_INDEX_SCHEDULE: '' PHOTOPRISM_AUTO_INDEX: 300 PHOTOPRISM_AUTO_IMPORT: -1 PHOTOPRISM_DETECT_NSFW: 'false' PHOTOPRISM_UPLOAD_NSFW: 'true' PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_DRIVER: mysql PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_SERVER: mariadb:3306 PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_NAME: photoprism PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_USER: photoprism PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_PASSWORD: insecure PHOTOPRISM_SITE_CAPTION: AI-Powered Photos App PHOTOPRISM_SITE_DESCRIPTION: '' PHOTOPRISM_SITE_AUTHOR: '' working_dir: /photoprism volumes: - ./pictures:/photoprism/originals - ./storage_data:/photoprism/storage container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:2342' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network mariadb: image: mariadb:11 restart: unless-stopped stop_grace_period: 5s security_opt: - seccomp:unconfined - apparmor:unconfined volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/mysql environment: MARIADB_AUTO_UPGRADE: '1' MARIADB_INITDB_SKIP_TZINFO: '1' MARIADB_DATABASE: photoprism MARIADB_USER: photoprism MARIADB_PASSWORD: insecure MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD: insecure container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mariadb' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mariadb # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
